content.problemStatement:
You are trying to create an array AAA containing NNN integers. However, your array-creating-machine has some restrictions. More formally, you are given a constant DDD, and two arrays BBB and CCC, each with MMM elements, then:


Ai‚Č§DA_i \le DAi‚Äč‚Č§D for all 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N
Ai‚Č•Ai‚ąí1A_i \ge A_{i - 1}Ai‚Äč‚Č•Ai‚ąí1‚Äč for all 1<i‚Č§N1 \lt i \le N1<i‚Č§N
ACj‚čÖBj‚Č§DA_{C_j}\cdot B_j \le DACj‚Äč‚Äč‚čÖBj‚Äč‚Č§D for all 1‚Č§j‚Č§M1 \le j \le M1‚Č§j‚Č§M
Find how many different arrays can you create. Since answer can be big, print it modulo
998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. The test cases then follow.
The first line of each test case contains 333 integers NNN, MMM, and DDD - the length of the array AAA you need to create, the length of BBB and CCC, the constant from the statement.
The iii-th line of the next MMM lines of each test case contains two integers CiC_iCi‚Äč and BiB_iBi‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case output a single integer - the number of different arrays you can create modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: It's guaranteed that the sum of MMM over all test cases doesn't exceed 10510^5105, and sum of DDD over all test cases doesn't exceed 10510^5105
It's guaranteed that in each test case all CiC_iCi‚Äč are different
**Subtask 111 (30 points)**: sum of DDD over all test cases doesn't exceed 300030003000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: One array is {1,‚ÄČ1,‚ÄČ1,‚ÄČ1}\{1,\,1,\,1,\,1\}{1,1,1,1} and for each value of last element from 222 to 666, there are 333 possible combinations of first three values: {1,‚ÄČ1,‚ÄČ1}\{1,\,1,\,1\}{1,1,1}, {1,‚ÄČ1,‚ÄČ2}\{1,\,1,\,2\}{1,1,2}, {1,‚ÄČ2,‚ÄČ2}\{1,\,2,\,2\}{1,2,2}. Therefore there are a total of 1+5‚čÖ3=161 + 5 \cdot 3 = 161+5‚čÖ3=16 possible arrays.
